title: Appointment & Resignation of Director Filing Online | DIR-12
metaDescription: Get appointment of director and resignation of director filing done online. Know director appointment process, director resignation forms & documents required for DIR-12 filing.

hero.headline: Appointment & Resignation of Director – DIR-12 Filing Process Online
hero.subtext: Professionals Law manages DIR-12 filing, resolutions, DSCs & MCA formalities – accurately and on time.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: Under the Companies Act, 2013, any appointment or resignation of a director must be officially reported to the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) by filing Form DIR-12 within 30 days of such change. Every company whether it is Private Limited, Public Limited or Section 8 Company has to file the same. DIR-12 is the form which needs to be filed in order to intimate the Registrar of Companies (ROC) on the correct constitution of the Board of a company. Professionals Law takes care of everything in a comprehensive manner by drafting essential board/shareholder resolutions, readying digital forms, getting digital signatures and filing DIR-12 within a time limit, thereby ensuring zero compliances and no legal penalty for not reporting the change of director or delayed reporting.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: Filing DIR-12 is a statutory requirement for notifying any change in the composition of a company’s Board of Directors. It provides for the legal effectiveness of appointments, resignations, re-appointments or removals and keeps up to date records to be delivered to the Registrar of Companies. Failure to file or delay in filing can result in penalties, prosecution and even disqualification of director(s). Professionals Law eases the burden, by handling all forms, and resolutions and documentation and MCA filing, so that your company stays in compliance and out of the penalty box.

body.title: Legal Compliance
body.description: Timely filing of DIR-12 ensures the company meets statutory compliance norms under the Companies Act, avoiding penalties and prosecution.

body.title: Transparency in Governance
body.description: Keeps the MCA database up to date with real-time changes in the company’s board, promoting transparent corporate governance.

body.title: Smooth Transition of Roles
body.description: Helps formalize the entry or exit of directors, ensuring there are no disputes regarding powers or responsibilities.

body.title: Stakeholder Trust
body.description: An accurate board structure improves stakeholder confidence and ensures alignment with lenders, investors and partners.

body.title: Support for Future Transactions
body.description: Essential for smooth audits, bank dealings, legal matters or if the company plans to raise capital or expand.

criteria: The individual being appointed must possess a valid DIN (Director Identification Number) and be at least 18 years of age.
Companies making the appointment/resignation must be registered under the Companies Act, 2013.
The appointee must not be disqualified under Section 164 of the Act.
For resignation, the director must submit a resignation letter and the company must accept it via Board Resolution.

documents: DIR-2: Consent to act as Director
Acknowledgement of Resignation by the Company

body.description: Hold a meeting to pass a resolution for appointment or resignation.
Get consent (DIR-2), declarations, resignation letter, etc.

body.step: Prepare and Digitally Sign Form DIR-12
body.description: Professionals Law will prepare DIR-12 with the necessary attachments and get it digitally signed.
Submit the form with all documents on the MCA portal within 30 days of the event.
After submission, receive the SRN and MCA acknowledgement as proof of compliance.

body.title: For Officer in Default
body.description: Fine of ₹50,000 and ₹500 per day of continuing default.

body.title: Disqualification Risk
body.description: MCA may also initiate disqualification proceedings under Section 164(2).

body.title: Filing Deadline
body.description: Within 30 days of appointment/resignation/change in designation, form DIR-12 shall be filed.

body.question: Is it mandatory to file DIR-12 for the resignation of a director?
body.answer: Yes, it is mandatory to file DIR-12 within 30 days from the resignation date.

body.question: Who can sign the DIR-12 form?
body.answer: It must be digitally signed by a director or company secretary and certified by a professional (CA/CS/CMA).

body.question: What is the timeline to file DIR-12 after appointment/resignation?
body.answer: The company must file DIR-12 within 30 days of the event.

body.question: Can Professionals Law help if the form is delayed?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law can assist in delayed filings and help calculate and pay any additional fees.

body.question: What if a company fails to file DIR-12?
body.answer: The company and its officers may be penalized and the director’s appointment/resignation won’t be reflected in MCA records.

body.question: Can I file resignation without Board approval?
body.answer: A director can resign by serving notice, but the Board must acknowledge it for legal closure.

body.question: What if the director’s DSC is expired?
body.answer: Professionals Law helps renew DSCs instantly to ensure timely DIR-12 filing.

body.question: Is shareholder approval always needed for appointments?
body.answer: Not always. Initial and casual vacancies may be filled by the Board, but shareholder approval may be needed later.

body.question: Does Professionals Law provide resolution drafts and consent letters?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law provides complete documentation support including board/shareholder resolutions, consent letters and declarations.
